phaseA_tabInventory.customTabs: Opus-generated tabs rendered dynamically from agent_run output

primaryDataSource.scenarioAwareness: INDIRECT — deal_assumptions.year1 is synced FROM deal_underwriting_scenarios via DB trigger trg_sync_underwriting_scenario on every scenario write; getDealFinancials always reads deal_assumptions

frontendMergeLayer.logic: When modelResults===null: clones f9Financials and rebuilds projections from assumptions client-side. When modelResults present: calls mergeModelIntoFinancials() which maps model.summary.irr→lpNetIrr, model.summary.equityMultiple→lpEquityMultiple, etc.
frontendMergeLayer.criticalGap: RC-001 (FIXED in this task): /financials route was overwriting getDealFinancials rich returns {lpNetIrr,lpEquityMultiple,avgCashOnCash,...} with simplified {irr,equityMultiple,cashOnCash}. When modelResults===null, lpNetIrr was undefined in mergedFinancials. Fix: route now passes data.returns through unchanged.

phaseC_rootCauses.title: Returns hero strip blank until cashflow model is run
phaseC_rootCauses.description: The /financials route handler (inline-deals.routes.ts) was overwriting getDealFinancials's rich returns object {lpNetIrr, lpEquityMultiple, avgCashOnCash, gpPromoteEarned, ...} with a simplified {irr, equityMultiple, cashOnCash} computed from a local computeIrr() bisection. The frontend mergeModelIntoFinancials maps model.summary.irr→lpNetIrr (not src.returns.irr→lpNetIrr). When modelResults===null (no cashflow model has been built), the useMemo early-returns a cloned f9Financials where returns.lpNetIrr===undefined. ReturnsTab reads ret?.lpNetIrr which was undefined→null→'—' for all 4 hero tiles.
affectedTabs: ReturnsTab (hero strip: LP NET IRR, LP EQUITY MULTIPLE, AVG CASH-ON-CASH, GP PROMOTE EARNED)

evidence.routeOverwrite: inline-deals.routes.ts — removed: let returns = { irr, equityMultiple, cashOnCash } (overrode data.returns)
evidence.mergeFunction: FinancialEnginePage.tsx:92 — out.returns.lpNetIrr = hasLvReturns ? src.returns.lpNetIrr : (s.lpIrr ?? s.irr ?? null) — s from model.summary not src.returns

fix.description: Removed 15-line simplified returns overwrite block from /financials route. Removed unused import buildProjectionsForExport and unused computeIrr() IRR bisection helper. data.returns from getDealFinancials (lpNetIrr, lpEquityMultiple, avgCashOnCash, gpPromoteEarned, debtMetrics, lpTrancheReturns) now flows through to the client unchanged.

phaseC_rootCauses.title: Other income data gap — year1.other_income key absent; other_income_dollars used instead
phaseC_rootCauses.scope: Bishop, Sentosa — deals where cashflow agent ran and wrote back other_income_dollars
phaseC_rootCauses.description: DB shows da.year1 has NO 'other_income' LayeredValue key — it is null. Instead, other_income_dollars carries the agent write-back value (Bishop: $341,907 annual; Sentosa: $0). getDealFinancials at line 2258 calls toDollarRow('other_income_per_unit','other_income','Other Income', multiplier, 'other_income_dollars') — the 5th argument instructs it to read other_income_dollars as the annual total source. The F-009 fix in financials-composer.service.ts confirms this intent but that function is only called on rent roll cell edits (PUT /financials/override), not the main /financials GET. getDealFinancials has its own parallel plumbing for this.
phaseC_rootCauses.proposedFix: Verify toDollarRow correctly resolves other_income_dollars.resolved for the display value; add integration test. No code change expected.

phaseC_rootCauses.title: Sentosa interest_rate NULL — debt service calculations produce zero
phaseC_rootCauses.description: deal_assumptions.interest_rate is NULL for Sentosa Epperson. No debt:senior:interest_rate entry in per_year_overrides either. This means capitalStack.interestRate=null, annual debt service = 0, DSCR = null (no debt service denominator), CFBt = NOI (uncorrected by debt), and the Returns IRR calculation uses equity=0 or wrong equity. The deal was never fully configured with financing terms.
phaseC_rootCauses.proposedFix: DATA: Configure Sentosa's senior loan terms via the Debt Advisor (M11 Configure sub-tab). Not a code bug.

phaseC_rootCauses.title: Real estate tax key normalized — F-HIGH-004 fix addresses cashflow model alias
phaseC_rootCauses.status: FIXED (F-HIGH-004 merged prior to this task)
phaseC_rootCauses.description: DB stores real estate tax under key 'real_estate_tax' (singular) in year1 JSONB. Cashflow postprocess was expecting 'expense.real_estate_taxes' (plural). F-HIGH-004 added KEY_ALIASES normalization in cashflow.postprocess.ts. Both deals have agent-written values: Bishop $540K, Sentosa $1.3M. getDealFinancials reads the 'real_estate_tax' key directly (correct singular form) for the non-model proforma display path, so this only affected the cashflow model path.
affectedTabs: ProjectionsTab RE Taxes row (cashflow model path)
phaseC_rootCauses.proposedFix: Already fixed. No further action needed.

phaseC_rootCauses.title: Waterfall/Capital sections blank — no LP tranches configured
phaseC_rootCauses.scope: All 4 test deals (and any deal without LP tranche configuration)
phaseC_rootCauses.description: The CapitalHubTab WaterfallTab and ReturnsTab §2 (LP Returns by Tranche) are blank because no LP tranches have been configured in the capital schedule. getDealFinancials returns waterfall={} and capital.tranches=[]. The frontend correctly shows 'No LP tranches configured. Configure in Capital →' prompt. This is a product UX gap, not a code bug.
affectedTabs: ReturnsTab §2 LP Returns by Tranche (shows 'No LP tranches configured')

evidence.ReturnsTab_guard: ReturnsTab.tsx:358 — if (lpTranches.length === 0) shows 'Configure in Capital' prompt

phaseC_rootCauses.proposedFix: NOT a code bug. Add default LP/GP tranche templates on deal creation to improve first-run experience. See follow-up Task #876.

phaseC_rootCauses.title: F-009 fix scope: composeDealFinancials vs getDealFinancials
phaseC_rootCauses.description: The F-009 fix was applied to financials-composer.service.ts (composeDealFinancials path). This path is called only on rent roll cell edits (PUT /financials/override). The main /financials GET uses getDealFinancials which has its own other_income_dollars mapping at line 2258. The fix is partially redundant — getDealFinancials already handled this. However, the composer path needed the fix for the override flow. Both paths should now be consistent.
affectedTabs: AssumptionsTab Other Income (via /financials GET = getDealFinancials path)
phaseC_rootCauses.proposedFix: Verify that getDealFinancials toDollarRow correctly picks up other_income_dollars.resolved. Add regression test.

phaseD_tabStatusMatrix.note: Status evaluated at time of audit post FIX-RC-001. 'WORKING' = populates with real data. 'BLANK' = shows — or empty state. 'PARTIAL' = some rows blank. Control-A = Westside Lofts (seeded, no scenario). Control-B = Highlands at Satellite (unseeded, no scenario).

phaseE_controlComparison.finding: Westside Lofts (Control-A: seeded, no active scenario) experiences the SAME RC-001 and RC-005 blank states as Bishop/Sentosa prior to the fix. This confirms: the Priority-0 scenario system (Task #869) does NOT introduce additional blank tabs. deal_assumptions is kept in sync with the active scenario via DB trigger trg_sync_underwriting_scenario. Blank tabs on Bishop/Sentosa were pre-existing issues unrelated to scenario introduction.
phaseE_controlComparison.highlands_finding: Highlands at Satellite (Control-B: unseeded, no active scenario) shows blank/default states across all tabs that require year1 data. This is expected behavior for a completely new unseeded deal and establishes the worst-case blank-tab baseline: ConsoleHub inputs, Projections revenue rows, Returns §1/hero strip, Decision tab are all BLANK. Sensitivity is the only tab that works independently of year1 data.
phaseE_controlComparison.scenarioRisk: If trg_sync_underwriting_scenario fails silently (e.g. trigger error, transaction rollback), scenario writes would not propagate to deal_assumptions and getDealFinancials would serve stale year1 data. No evidence this is happening — both Priority-0 deals show consistent GPR values between scenario and deal_assumptions.

mainFinancialsEndpoint.finding: getDealFinancials reads deal_assumptions which mirrors active scenario via trg_sync_underwriting_scenario. No direct scenario_id parameter required or used.

narrativeEndpoint.finding: Narrative is cached per dealId, not per scenario. If scenario changes significantly, narrative may be stale until 24h cache expires or ?refresh=true is passed.

patchEndpoints.finding: PATCH /assumptions/* routes write to active deal_underwriting_scenarios (Task #869 implementation) then DB trigger syncs back to deal_assumptions.

leaseVelocity.finding: POST /lease-velocity/run is a standalone calculation; does not read from scenario or deal_assumptions.

prioritizedActionItems.action: Stop the /financials route from overwriting data.returns — let getDealFinancials rich returns flow through
prioritizedActionItems.impact: Eliminates blank Returns hero strip on all deals without model run

prioritizedActionItems.action: Configure Sentosa senior loan terms: interest_rate, loan_amount via Debt Advisor or direct DB seed
prioritizedActionItems.impact: Restores Sentosa debt service rows, DSCR, CFBt, LTV

prioritizedActionItems.action: Verify Returns tab hero strip shows real numbers on first load (post FIX-RC-001) and add smoke test assertion
prioritizedActionItems.impact: Confirms FIX-RC-001 effective; confirms other_income_dollars path works

prioritizedActionItems.action: Seed default LP/GP tranche on deal creation (e.g. 80/20 LP/GP split at 8% pref)
prioritizedActionItems.impact: Eliminates blank Waterfall/LP tranche sections on first-run for all deals
